examples:
!xfe-search-cves q="Heartbleed"
Context Example
#
{
"CVE": [
{
"CVSS": "2.0",
"Description": "IBM WebSphere Application Server is not vulnerable to the Heartbleed vulnerability (CVE-2014-0160) where secure data might not be protected. However, there is a potential denial of service on IBM WebSphere Application Server Version 6.1 and 6.0.2 when running the Heartbleed scanning tools or if sending specially-crafted Heartbeat messages.",

outputs.description: The ID of the CVE. CVE.CVSS The CVSS of the CVE.
outputs.name: CVE.ID

outputs.description: Date The date this was published. CVE.Description
outputs.name: CVE.Published

outputs.description: The CVE description. XFE.CVE.Xfdbid The XFBID of the CVE.
outputs.name: Unknown

outputs.description: The risk level of the CVE. XFE.CVE.Reported Date
outputs.name: XFE.CVE.RiskLevel

outputs.description: XFE.CVE.Cvss The CVSS information of the CVE. XFE.CVE.Stdcode
outputs.name: The reported date of the CVE.

outputs.description: The CVE stdcodes. XFE.CVE.Title The title of the CVE.
outputs.name: Unknown

outputs.description: The description of the CVE. XFE.CVE.PlatformsAffected The affetcted platforms due to the CVE.
outputs.name: XFE.CVE.Description

outputs.description: The exploitability of the CVE. XFE.CVESearch.TotalRows The total rows received after search.
outputs.name: XFE.CVE.Exploitability

outputs.description: Bookmark used to page through results.
outputs.name: XFE.CVESearch.Bookmark
